creator: mike@jellydonut.org
text:
After completing the xorg 7.3 upgrade with direction from /usr/ports/UPDATING, there appears to be a problem with the x11/aterm port, where transparency no longer works and the terminal has all manner of garbled character artifacts when attempting to use coloring and/or transparency within an aterm window.

Running aterm from a separate terminal process yields the following errors on screen:

aterm has encountered the following problem interacting with X Windows :
      Request: 64,    Error: 8(BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes))
      in resource: 0x1C0000E
.. [this same resource address is repeated 11 times]

aterm has encountered the following problem interacting with X Windows :
      Request: 64,    Error: 8(BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes))
      in resource: 0x1C00015
.. [this same resource address is repeated 103 times]

aterm has encountered the following problem interacting with X Windows :
      Request: 64,    Error: 8(BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes))
      in resource: 0x1C00019
.. [this same resource address is repeated 103 times]

aterm has encountered the following problem interacting with X Windows :
      Request: 70,    Error: 8(BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes))
      in resource: 0x1C00021
aterm has encountered the following problem interacting with X Windows :
      Request: 67,    Error: 8(BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes))
      in resource: 0x1C00021
aterm has encountered the following problem interacting with X Windows :
      Request: 70,    Error: 8(BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes))
      in resource: 0x1C00021
aterm has encountered the following problem interacting with X Windows :
      Request: 62,    Error: 8(BadMatch (invalid parameter attributes))
      in resource: 0x1C00009


I have tried doing a forced rebuild of both graphics/libafterstep and x11/aterm and all dependencies after the upgrade, with the same results.

Fix: 

unknown
How-To-Repeat: 1) update ports tree to 2007-09-17
2) portupgrade -R xorg
3) run "aterm" in a xterm window
